scenario_results.notes: Covers normal dependency and calendar design using scheduler-centric controls.
Covers legacy-hybrid coexistence and modernization path sequencing.
Covers restart, rerun, and recovery boundaries without guessing application internals.
Covers critical-path prioritization and operator escalation behavior.
Covers scheduler agent failure and dependency safety checks.
Covers migration from native schedulers into enterprise workload automation.
Covers calendar drift, regional exceptions, and governance handling.
Verifies rejection of adjacent file-transfer and EDI work.
Verifies rejection of adjacent scripting and task automation work.
Verifies clarification and escalation instead of false certainty.
Covers migration-risk prioritization for undocumented legacy scheduler estates.
Verifies fallback-safe operation when shared meta-agent responses are stale.

notes: Scenario suite exceeds the minimum count and covers normal guidance, legacy constraints, failure modes, migration risk, boundary rejection, and low-confidence escalation.
Results are evidence-backed against the authority baseline and show no unreviewed regressions.
This package records specialist-owned content plus fallback-safe delegated meta-agent contracts required by issue #151.
